title: Security Driver Control Structure (USecDrv)
text: Security Driver Control Structure (USecDrv) The security driver control structure is used to exchange information between the driver and the caller. Description The security driver structure contains some members that are reserved for Uniface use only, and must not be used. Members The following table lists the members of the structure that can be used by the security driver. Table 1. Security Driver Structure Members Type Name Description C/S SMTP void * DrvArea Can be used by the implementation to allocate memory for driver-specific information or state, and store the pointer. The implementation is responsible for freeing any memory that it allocates. X X unsigned char * Username Points to the start of the null-terminated user name. X unsigned char * Passwd Points to the start of the null-terminated user password. X unsigned char * NewPasswd Points to the start of the null-terminated user new password. X void * UserToken Points to a user token, required on Microsoft Windows platforms to create a process for the given (authenticated) user. X unsigned char * InBuf Points to the start of the input buffer, that contains the information to be encoded or decoded. X X int InBufLen Length (in bytes) of the buffer pointed to by InBuf . X X int InDataLen Length (in bytes) of the data stored in the buffer pointed to by InBuf . X X unsigned char * OutBuf Points to the start of the output buffer, that contains the result of encoding or decoding the data in the input buffer InBuf . X X int OutBufLen Length (in bytes) of the buffer pointed to by OutBuf . X X int OutDataLen Length (in bytes) of the data stored in the buffer pointed to by OutBuf . X X unsigned char * ErrMsg Points to the buffer containing the implementation-specific error text (or info message text) that describes the supplied error number (or info message number) contained in Error . This buffer is guaranteed to be at least 256 bytes. X X int ErrMsgLen Length (in bytes) of the implementation-specific error text (or info message text) in the buffer pointed to by ErrMsg . X X long Error Implementation-specific error message number (or info message number) returned by the security driver. If it encounters an error, it should also return a status from the entry point function indicating the severity of the error. This member is only viewed when the entry point function return status is not successful (not USEC_SUCCESS ). It is this number for which text must be supplied in the ErrMsg buffer. X X unsigned char * Function Specifies the action to be taken by the security driver, as listed in Security Driver Functions. X X unsigned char * Properties SMTP driver characteristics, provided in the first parameter of the SetSecurity operation of the UPOPMAIL component. It consists of a Uniface list containing the following keyword-value pairs: SMTP= EntryPointFunction —function that implements the SMTP security driver; can be upper or lowercase, and is case-sensitive. USE= OPT | MAND —indicates whether use of this driver is optional or mandatory OPT—optional, if the server supports this functionality; MAND—mandatory (default); if the server does not support it, an error must be returned. The keyword and values may be upper or lower case. This string is in the UTF-8 character set. X unsigned char * Parameters SMTP driver-specific parameters, provided in the second parameter of the SetSecurity operation of the UPOPMAIL component. It consists of a Uniface list in the UTF-8 character set. See the documentation for a specific driver for the keywords and values that may be present. Drivers can always use the DrvArea member to allocate space for their own administrations. The pointer is preserved over calls and it is the driver's responsibility to free the memory. X Related Links Security Driver Functions A security driver must perform the action requested of it, as defined by the Function member of the USecDrv structure. Some actions are applicable to both client/server and SMTP security drivers, others are specific to one or the other. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
